---
# FRONT MATTER — page-level settings (override _quarto.yml)
title: "SORTEE Code Club"
subtitle: "Open & reproducible coding for ecologists and evolutionary biologists"

# title-block-banner gives the title area a coloured background
title-block-banner: true

# Do you remember how toc was "true" in the yaml? We can override it for each page!
toc: false
---

## 📣 Announcements

<!-- CALLOUT BLOCKS
     Quarto has 5 types: note, tip, warning, important, caution
     They render with icons and colour automatically — no CSS needed.
     Add a title with ## inside, or omit it for a plain callout.     -->

::: {.callout-note}
## Next Session: June 23, 2026 · 09:00 UTC

**Topic:** TADA-SORTEE code guidelines with [Ed Ivimey-Cook](https://eivimeycook.github.io/)

Interested? [Become a SORTEE Member!](https://sortee.org/join/){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 💡 New to the club? Start here

1. Join [SORTEE](https://sortee.org/join/){target="_blank"} to get session links
2. Check out the [resources page](resources.qmd) for beginner-friendly guides
3. No previous coding experience needed — all skill levels welcome!

:::


## Who We Are

<!-- COLUMNS LAYOUT
     Pure Quarto syntax — works with any theme, no CSS needed.
     Width values should add up to 100%.                          -->

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

SORTEE Code Club is part of the [Society for Open, Reliable, and Transparent
Ecology and Evolutionary Biology (SORTEE)](https://www.sortee.org){target="_blank"}.

We meet every month to learn, share, and improve our coding skills.
Whether you're debugging your first R script or building a Shiny app,
there's a place for you here.

We cover:

- 🐍 R and Python for ecological data analysis
- 📊 Data visualisation with ggplot2 and beyond
- 🔁 Reproducible workflows with Quarto and RMarkdown
- 🌐 Open science tools: OSF, GitHub, Zenodo

:::


:::





## A Little Reproducible Science 🔬

The plot below is generated from R code embedded directly in this page — this is what makes Quarto powerful!


```{r}
#| label: demo-plot
#| echo: true                # show the code (overrides global echo: false)
#| fig-cap: "Simulated species richness across a precipitation gradient"
#| fig-alt: "Scatter plot showing a positive relationship between precipitation and species richness"
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

library(ggplot2)

set.seed(42)
n <- 80
data <- data.frame(precipitation_mm = runif(n, 200, 1800),
                   temperature_c    = runif(n, 5, 30))

data$species_richness <- round(0.05 * data$precipitation_mm - 0.8  * data$temperature_c +
                                 rnorm(n, 0, 8) + 20)

ggplot(data, aes(x = precipitation_mm, y = species_richness, colour = temperature_c)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "#2C9B8A", high = "#E07B39",
                        name = "Temperature (°C)") +
  labs(x = "Annual precipitation (mm)",
       y = "Species richness",
       caption = "Simulated data for demonstration") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)
```


## Get Involved

<!-- TABSET
     Great for organising related content without a long scroll.
     Each ### heading becomes a tab label.                        -->

::: {.panel-tabset}

### 🎤 Volunteer to Present

We're always looking for people to share their coding knowledge —
beginners welcome! You could run a:

- **Tutorial** (30–45 min): teach a skill you've recently learned
- **Code review** (20 min): share a workflow for group feedback
- **Demo** (10 min): show a package or tool you like

[Express interest →](mailto:codeclub@sortee.org){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

### 🛠 Suggest a Topic

Missing something from our schedule?
Tell us what you'd like to learn.

Current wishlist:

1. Bayesian statistics in R (Stan / brms)
2. Spatial data with `sf` and `terra`
3. Machine learning for ecologists
4. Python for R users

[Open an issue on GitHub →](https://github.com/SORTEE/CodeClub/issues){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}

:::

---

*SORTEE Code Club is a volunteer-run community committed to a welcoming,
inclusive environment for all skill levels.
Read our [Code of Conduct](https://sortee.org/codeofconduct/).*
